title: contiguous does not work with one non-singleton dimension
issue_summary: The fast attention path rejected tensors after contiguous when the last dimension was singleton and therefore had a stride different from one, even though such singleton-dimension stride layouts are valid.

root_cause.summary: The backend validator treated last-dimension stride equal to one as a universal requirement instead of exempting singleton dimensions.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: Fast or memory-efficient attention falls back or errors out on tensors whose last dimension has size one and stride greater than one.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case constructs a tensor view with element strides [1, 2] and singleton last dimension, then compares the buggy validator that rejects it with the corrected validator that accepts singleton-dimension layouts.

issue_contract.trigger: Send a tensor whose last dimension is singleton but has stride greater than one to the fast attention validator.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy validator requires last-dimension stride 1 even when the last dimension has size 1.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: A valid tensor layout is rejected by the fast attention backend.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces the overly strict kernel stride validator on singleton trailing dimensions.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported observable symptom that the fast attention path rejects an otherwise valid input layout.
